rules: Show expert testing first when available.
Show exact YouTube videos and exact Reddit threads before any broader discovery notes.
Use brand and specs pages to confirm product details, not as proof that a product deserves to win.
Keep editorial citations separate from shopping and affiliate links.
Keep generic discovery URLs internal until an exact public source is curated.

globalTierLabels.tier1: Strong category-specific testing or a specialist source with clear hands-on context.
globalTierLabels.tier2: Useful editorial or creator coverage that supports the recommendation but is less specialized.
globalTierLabels.tier3: Community, owner, or supporting context that helps expose long-term tradeoffs.

tierLabels.tier1: Specialist running, hiking, and outdoor testing with real trail or field use.
tierLabels.tier2: Useful creator or general gear coverage with product-specific impressions.
tierLabels.tier3: Runner and owner discussions that surface fit, durability, and terrain caveats.

tierLabels.tier1: Hands-on office, ergonomics, or hardware testing with setup and durability notes.
tierLabels.tier2: Credible workplace gear coverage with product-specific context.
tierLabels.tier3: Owner discussion that helps identify wobble, comfort, assembly, and long-term issues.

tierLabels.tier1: Hands-on home, travel, or wellness testing with clear scoring or use-case notes.
tierLabels.tier2: Credible editorial coverage that clarifies usability, value, or maintenance.
tierLabels.tier3: Owner discussion that helps reveal reliability, noise, cleaning, fit, or long-term satisfaction.
